name: Forget Hide Archive Destructive Action Semantics
purpose: Define exact privacy action semantics so forget, hide, archive, and destructive action boundaries cannot be confused or used unsafely by Carnos, dashboards, analytics, exports, connectors, or future UI actions.

schema_requirement.reason: 20D defines action semantics and safety boundaries only. It references existing memory, audit, retention, and readiness sources without creating or modifying tables, policies, migrations, or live execution code.
schema_requirement.future_schema_gate: If a later chunk creates privacy_action_requests, manifests, retention rows, audit rows, or hard-removal execution, inspect exact schema before coding.

action_definitions.label: Forget
action_definitions.definition: Remove the item from Carnos use and active memory context. The item may remain in protected records, audit history, or source history depending on existing retention rules.
action_definitions.carnos_behavior: Carnos cannot read, summarize, retrieve, rank, cite, personalize from, or use the forgotten item in context.
action_definitions.dashboard_behavior: Normal dashboards must not treat forgotten memory as active memory.
action_definitions.analytics_behavior: Analytics must not use forgotten memory as active personalization input.
action_definitions.export_behavior: Export preview must mark forgotten status and follow redaction/retention rules.
action_definitions.audit_behavior: Forgetting must create an audit event.
action_definitions.confirmation_level: review required for sensitive or bulk actions

action_definitions.label: Hide
action_definitions.definition: Remove the item from broad user-facing surfaces without necessarily removing it from storage or all internal references.
action_definitions.user_visible_meaning: Do not show this on normal dashboards, timelines, cards, summaries, or broad search surfaces.
action_definitions.carnos_behavior: Carnos access depends on separate Carnos permission and lock state.
action_definitions.dashboard_behavior: Broad dashboards and timelines must not show hidden items.
action_definitions.analytics_behavior: Analytics may use hidden items only when privacy level, sensitivity level, and lock state allow.
action_definitions.export_behavior: Export preview must mark hidden status.
action_definitions.audit_behavior: Hiding must create an audit event.
action_definitions.confirmation_level: single review for normal data, stronger review for sensitive data

action_definitions.label: Archive
action_definitions.definition: Remove the item from active use while preserving it for history, proof, audit, or long-term recordkeeping.
action_definitions.user_visible_meaning: Keep it for history, but stop treating it as active.
action_definitions.carnos_behavior: Carnos cannot use archived memory for active personalization unless explicitly allowed by a future narrow rule.
action_definitions.dashboard_behavior: Active dashboards should not show archived items unless an archive/history surface is selected.
action_definitions.analytics_behavior: Analytics should exclude archived items from active trend calculations unless using an archive/history mode.
action_definitions.export_behavior: Export preview must mark archived status.
action_definitions.audit_behavior: Archiving must create an audit event.

action_definitions.label: Destructive Action
action_definitions.definition: A protected high-risk boundary for hard-removal or irreversible removal-style operations. It is not the default meaning of forget, hide, or archive.
action_definitions.user_visible_meaning: High-risk removal action that needs stronger confirmation and audit.
action_definitions.carnos_behavior: Carnos cannot execute or approve destructive actions.
action_definitions.dashboard_behavior: UI must show preview, affected records, skipped records, confirmation requirement, cooldown when applicable, and final status.
action_definitions.analytics_behavior: Analytics must not assume destructive action completed until manifest status says completed.
action_definitions.export_behavior: Export preview must show destructive action history where audit retention allows.
action_definitions.audit_behavior: Every destructive action request, confirmation, block, completion, or failure must be audited.
action_definitions.confirmation_level: two-step confirmation plus cooldown where required

derived_record_rules: Derived records must not remain active if their source memory is forgotten.
Derived records must be marked stale, blocked, redacted, or removed from active use according to existing readiness rules.
Derived records should keep source lineage where audit and retention allow.
Carnos cannot use derived records that depend on forgotten memories.
Analytics cannot use derived records that depend on forgotten memories for active personalization.
Export preview must identify affected derived record categories when available.
Hard-removal of derived records is deferred to a protected boundary and cannot be silently executed.
review_and_confirmation_rules: Normal hide can use single review unless the item is sensitive or locked.
Normal archive can use single review unless the item is sensitive or locked.
Forget requires review for memory, sensitive data, Carnos-visible data, or bulk scope.
Destructive action always requires two-step confirmation.
Destructive action may require cooldown depending on risk level.
Fully locked data cannot be downgraded or acted on without explicit review.
Carnos cannot approve any forget or destructive action.
carnos_rules: Carnos may explain the difference between forget, hide, archive, and destructive action.
Carnos may propose a privacy action only when the domain allows proposals.
Carnos cannot approve a privacy action.
Carnos cannot execute a destructive action.
Carnos cannot use fully locked data.
Carnos cannot treat archive as active memory.
Carnos cannot silently create hard-removal requests.
must_not_do: do not invent live privacy_action_requests schema in 20D
do not implement hard-removal execution in 20D
do not let forget mean hard removal by default
do not let hide mean Carnos block by default unless Carnos permission says so
do not let archive remain active memory by default
do not let Carnos approve or execute destructive actions
do not remove or weaken audit boundaries
do not skip source lineage behavior
do not skip derived record behavior
acceptance: Destructive action manifest requirements are listed.
Review and confirmation rules are listed.
